articles.title: Morning recap
articles.summary: Morning recap Good morning Middle East Eye readers, Iran insisted on Saturday it had "kept its word" on a ceasefire with the United States, after President Donald Trump insisted the truce was over but that he had agreed to further negotiations with the Islamic republic. Iran’s foreign minister Abbas Aragchi said, "Violation follows other violations and missteps by the United States. Reality check: There can only be mutual compliance. Official: Iran suffered $300 million in damage to research infrastructure
articles.summary: Official: Iran suffered $300 million in damage to research infrastructure Iran’s vice president for Science, Technology and Knowledge-Based Economy Hossein Afshin said knowledge-based companies also suffered heavy losses, Iranian media reported. Afshin said knowledge-based firms incurred losses of 65 million rials and $25 million in foreign exchange during the "imposed war", IRNA reported. "The enemy had targeted Iran's scientific and technological authority, which will be rebuilt with power," Afshin said.

articles.title: IEA: US-Iran escalation threatens 2027 oil surplus forecast
articles.summary: IEA: US-Iran escalation threatens 2027 oil surplus forecast The International Energy Agency (IEA) predicts supply will expand by 7.5 million bpd next year after a 3.7 million bpd contraction this year, but that is contingent on improved Hormuz transits. "An escalation in hostilities on 7-8 July, however, clouds the outlook and could upend the forecast that sees the market flipping to a surplus next year," it said, adding that a lasting peace agreement is a "must" for oil markets to normalise, according to Reuters. The settlers also targeted their livestock, the QNN posted on X.  There has been a surge in settler attacks in the West Bank. Two Palestinian men, aged 28 and 48, were injured after being beaten during an Israeli settler attack on local lands in Beit Furik area of Nablus. articles.summary: Trump says US ready to 'completely decimate' Iran if he is targeted The US President warned Iran that the US military was prepared to "completely decimate and destroy" the country if Tehran carried out or attempted an assassination against him. In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that "1,000 missiles are locked and loaded" and aimed at Iran, with "thousands more" ready to follow if the Iranian government acted on what he described as threats made "in many corners of the globe" to assassinate the sitting US president. articles.summary: Report: Centcom team in Beirut to help implement Lebanon-Israel deal A US military delegation arrived in Lebanon on Friday to help implement an agreement to end the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, the Financial Times has reported, citing Lebanese officials. The US Central Command (Centcom) delegation’s visit to Lebanon comes ahead of talks between Lebanon and Israel in Rome next week, where technical teams are expected to discuss details of the framework agreement signed on June 26. articles.title: Israel bans Jerusalem Grand Mufti from Al-Aqsa Mosque for a week
articles.summary: Israel bans Jerusalem Grand Mufti from Al-Aqsa Mosque for a week Israeli forces first detained Sheikh Mohammad Hussein, the Grand Mufti of Jerusalem and Palestine and preacher of Al-Aqsa Mosque, after he concluded Friday sermon and prayers, WAFA reported. Following his detention, Israeli authorities handed Sheikh Hussein an order barring him from entering the Al-Aqsa Mosque complex for one week. In August last year, Israeli authorities issued a six-month ban on Sheikh Hussein, after he delivered a Friday sermon condemning Israel’s starvation policy against Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.&

articles.title: Report: Traffic in Hormuz falls sharply, only six vessels passed on 9-10 July night
articles.summary: Report: Traffic in Hormuz falls sharply, only six vessels passed on 9-10 July night Ship-tracking firm Windward said just six vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz on the night of 9-10 July, compared with 18 to 22 a few days ago. This was the third night of reduced traffic in the critical waterway, where Iranian attacks on ships led to a renewed wave of US strikes and Iranian counterattacks on the neighbouring Gulf nations. Outbound sailings have nearly stopped, just one vessel last night, against 15-21 on 5-7 July, Windward said in a post on X. Windward said “dark transits”, where vessels turn

articles.summary: Lebanon death toll rises to 4,321, health ministry says The Lebanese Health Ministry announced it has recorded 4,321 deaths and 12,207 injuries due to the Israeli military attacks on Lebanon since 2 March 2026. On Friday, an Israeli combat drone struck a vehicle in the town of Kfar Roumman, killing a Lebanese man and injuring another, according to the National News Agency (NNA). Another Israeli strike targeted a vehicle in Nabatieh town, injuring two civilians.

articles.title: Third UNSC meet on Iran nuclear file yields no resolution
articles.summary: Third UNSC meet on Iran nuclear file yields no resolution The UN Security Council met on Friday for a session since Resolution 2231 technically expired last October, with Russia and China once again forcing a procedural vote before any discussion could begin. The meeting, requested by Bahrain and the Council's five European members--Denmark, France, Greece, Latvia and the UK-- was held under the "Non-Proliferation" agenda item. articles.summary: US issues fresh Iran-related sanctions The US issued new Iran-related sanctions on Friday targeting a key financier for Iran's new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei and 13 other individuals and entities, following Tehran's resumed attacks on oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, the Treasury Department said. The sanctions took aim at Ali Ansari, an Iranian banker and businessman based in Dubai who had previously been sanctioned by Britain for his role in financially supporting the activities of Iran's Revolutionary Guards and other entities, Treasury said.
